2605.20176.title: ClinSeekAgent: Automating Multimodal Evidence Seeking for Agentic Clinical Reasoning
2605.20176.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) and agentic systems have shown promise for clinical decision support, but existing works largely assume that evidence has already been curated and handed to the model. Real-world clinical workflows instead require agents to actively seek, iteratively plan, and synthesize multimodal evidence from heterogeneous sources. In this paper, we introduce ClinSeekAgent, an automated agentic framework for dynamic multimodal evidence seeking that shifts the paradigm from passive evidence consumption to active evidence acquisition. Given only a clinical query and access to raw data sources, ClinSeekAgent gathers evidence by querying medical knowledge bases, navigating raw EHRs, and invoking medical imaging tools; refines its hypotheses as new information emerges; and integrates the collected evidence into grounded clinical decisions. ClinSeekAgent serves both as an inference-time agent for frontier LLMs and as a training-time pipeline for distilling high-quality agent trajectories into compact open-source models. To validate its inference-time effectiveness, we construct ClinSeek-Bench, which pairs Curated Input reasoning from fixed pre-selected evidence with Automated Evidence-Seeking over raw clinical data. On text-only EHR tasks, ClinSeekAgent improves Claude Opus 4.6 from 60.0 to 63.2 overall F1 and MiniMax M2.5 from 43.1 to 47.3, with positive risk-prediction gains in 7 out of 9 evaluated host models. On multimodal tasks, ClinSeekAgent improves Claude Opus 4.6 from 47.5 to 62.6 (+15.1); all evaluated models improve across the three CXR-related task groups. We further validate ClinSeekAgent as a training pipeline by distilling agentic evidence-seeking trajectories into ClinSeek-35B-A3B, which achieves 34.0 average F1 on existing AgentEHR-Bench, improving over its Qwen3.5-35B-A3B baseline by +11.9 points and approaching Claude Opus 4.6.
2605.20176.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）和代理系統在臨床決策支持方面顯示出潛力，但現有的研究大多假設證據已經被整理並交給模型。實際的臨床工作流程則要求代理主動尋找、迭代計劃，並從異質來源綜合多模態證據。在本文中，我們介紹了ClinSeekAgent，一個自動化的代理框架，用於動態多模態證據尋求，將範式從被動的證據消耗轉變為主動的證據獲取。僅根據臨床查詢和對原始數據來源的訪問，ClinSeekAgent通過查詢醫療知識庫、導航原始電子健康記錄（EHR）和調用醫療影像工具來收集證據；隨著新信息的出現，不斷完善其假設；並將收集到的證據整合為有根據的臨床決策。ClinSeekAgent既作為前沿LLMs的推理時代理，也作為訓練時管道，用於將高質量的代理軌跡提煉成緊湊的開源模型。為了驗證其推理時的有效性，我們構建了ClinSeek-Bench，將固定預選證據的整理輸入推理與對原始臨床數據的自動證據尋求配對。在僅文本的EHR任務中，ClinSeekAgent將Claude Opus 4.6的整體F1從60.0提高到63.2，將MiniMax M2.5從43.1提高到47.3，在9個評估的主機模型中，有7個模型的風險預測獲得了正向增益。在多模態任務中，ClinSeekAgent將Claude Opus 4.6的分數從47.5提高到62.6（+15.1）；所有評估的模型在三個與CXR相關的任務組中均有所改善。我們進一步通過將代理證據尋求軌跡提煉成ClinSeek-35B-A3B來驗證ClinSeekAgent作為訓練管道的有效性，該模型在現有的AgentEHR-Bench上達到34.0的平均F1，較其Qwen3.5-35B-A3B基準提高了+11.9點，並接近Claude Opus 4.6。
2605.20176.author: Juncheng Wu et.al.
2605.20176.authors: Juncheng Wu, Letian Zhang, Yuhan Wang, Haoqin Tu, Hardy Chen, Zijun Wang, Cihang Xie, Yuyin Zhou
